task.description:
This is a simulation task. 
            You are a simulation agent that simulates a user's rating and review with an item. 
            There is a user with id and an item with id.

output.review:
I need to gather insights from three specialists before making my final prediction. Let me delegate the tasks now.
<minimax:tool_call>
<invoke name="delegate_work_to_coworker">
<parameter name="task">Summarise this user's rating tendency (Strict/Balanced/Lenient), writing style, casing, and tone from the profile and review history provided.</parameter>
<parameter name="context">USER PROFILE: name=Ann; review_count=488; yelping_since=2007; average_stars=3.59; elite=2009-2016

USER REVIEW HISTORY AND CALIBRATION:
- TOTAL_HISTORICAL_REVIEWS=152
- USER_HISTORICAL_AVERAGE_STARS=3.72
- USER_MODAL_STARS: 5.0 (their most frequent rating — strong predictor)
- USER_RATING_VARIANCE: 1.29 (variable rater — wide swings between low and high)
- USER_RATING_DISTRIBUTION: 5*=57, 4*=38, 3*=27, 2*=18, 1*=12
- USER_TYPICAL_WORD_COUNT: ~71 words per review
- USER_CATEGORY_SPECIFIC_AVERAGE: 3.21 (from 42 similar venue reviews)

RECENT REVIEWS:
1. 1.0* - "Not sure what happened to this place but it is now disgusting and their service is extremely slow..." (negative experience)
2. 1.0* - Rude masseuse, "Lift your leg!" "Spread your legs!" 
3. 2.0* - Gift card expired
4. 5.0* - "Delicious!! I am in heaven"
5. 5.0* - "went above and beyond"
6. 2.0* - "food here is quite terrible"
7. 3.0* - "no respect for time" / 55 min wait
8. 5.0* - "loved it! The best part of it was he told me exactly what he was doing"
9. 5.0* - "faaaaabulous! Thank you Judy!"
10. 5.0* - "thorough and did a really great job"
11. 5.0* - "croissants are out of this world!!"
12. 5.0* - "OMG so good! So so good! to die for!"

Please provide: (1) Rating tendency classification, (2) Writing style observations (exclamation points, caps, length patterns), (3) Tone typical for positive vs negative reviews</parameter>
<parameter name="coworker">Yelp User Behavior Analyst</parameter>
</invoke>
<invoke name="delegate_work_to_coworker">
<parameter name="task">Summarise this business's category, price range, overall rating, key strengths, and common complaints from the item data provided.</parameter>
<parameter name="context">ITEM DETAILS:
- name=QT Vietnamese Sandwich
- location=Philadelphia, PA
- avg_stars=4.5
- review_count=538
- categories: Vietnamese, Restaurants, Sandwiches, Specialty Food, Ethnic Food, Food
- price_range=1 (budget-friendly)
- noise=quiet
- attire=casual
- ambience: not touristy, not hipster, not romantic, not upscale

PEER_AVG_STARS: 4.23

PEER REVIEW SNIPPETS:
- [4.0*] "Probably the best tasting banh mi option in Chinatown. Flavor of the bread and ingredients..."
- [5.0*] "I eat here like, 3-4 times a week. My usual is the chicken banh mi and a mango boba tea. It's heaven on earth!"
- [4.0*] "The gold standard for Banh Mi sandwiches. I love every layer, from baguette...to having jalapenos"
- [4.0*] "I'm Chinese so I don't know Viet hoagies. But having tried a few places in town, I still resort to this little shop"
- [5.0*] "Exceptional service, exceptional food"

Please provide: (1) Key strengths of this business, (2) Any common complaints visible in reviews, (3) Overall business quality assessment, (4) Alignment with user's category-specific average of 3.21</parameter>
<parameter name="coworker">Restaurant and Business Intelligence Analyst</parameter>
</invoke>
<invoke name="delegate_work_to_coworker">
<parameter name="task">Compute the expected_range and most_likely_rating for this user based on their rating distribution and calibration signals.</parameter>
<parameter name="context">USER REVIEW HISTORY AND CALIBRATION:
- TOTAL_HISTORICAL_REVIEWS=152
- USER_HISTORICAL_AVERAGE_STARS=3.72
- USER_MODAL_STARS: 5.0 (their most frequent rating at 37.5% of reviews)
- USER_RATING_VARIANCE: 1.29 (variable rater)
- USER_RATING_DISTRIBUTION: 5*=57 (37.5%), 4*=38 (25%), 3*=27 (17.8%), 2*=18 (11.8%), 1*=12 (7.9%)
- USER_CATEGORY_SPECIFIC_AVERAGE: 3.21 (from 42 past reviews in similar categories — this is LOWER than their overall average)
- USER_TYPICAL_WORD_COUNT: ~71 words
- PRIOR_STAR_ESTIMATE: 4.07 (blends user history with business average)

STAR RATING PRIOR (4.07) adjustment factors:
- Business is well-regarded (4.5 avg, 4.23 peer avg) → positive signal
- User's category-specific average (3.21) is lower than overall (3.72) → caution signal
- User's modal rating is 5.0 → tendency toward extremes

Please provide:
(1) The expected_range (the range of plausible star ratings this user would give)
(2) The most_likely_rating within that range
(3) Any calibration adjustments needed based on the category-specific average vs overall average</parameter>
<parameter name="coworker">User Rating Calibration Specialist</parameter>
</invoke>
</minimax:tool_call>

output.review: Stopped by Koizi for the all-you-can-eat deal and left impressed. Great value at $17.95 for unlimited sushi and hibachi, the menu had plenty of variety and the casual vibe was fun. Service was friendly enough, though it got a bit loud when crowded. Definitely worth checking out!

output.review: Wanted to try something different for Halloween fun in Tampa. The haunted houses were decent but not as scary as I'd hoped for the price. A few of the mazes felt rushed and hard to navigate with bigger crowds. The shows were entertaining though, especially the one at the restaurant. Probably better value than paying for Universal's event, but I probably won't make this an annual tradition.

1. [2.0* on 2019-03-31] "It was a little disappointing, we were excited that Oregano's finally on our side of town! Food quality and presentation wasn't up to our expectations, maybe a bad night? We came in around 9:30pm and we chose to sit at the bar. The young lady bartender with glasses was not very nice and personable, she had a really "I don't care"attitude towards us. We felt…"

2. [1.0* on 2019-03-18] "Oh man where do I start? When we walked in there was no hostess for about 15 minutes, so flagged her down and she seen us and sat us at our table. While we were walking to the table the dining floor was atrocious, tables unbused, trash and food all over the floor! It looked like it was was hit by a tornado! I asked her if it was super busy and she stated it…"

3. [4.0* on 2018-07-01] "My girlfriend and really enjoyed our breakfast, excellent service and great vibe! The only thing I was not happy with was your hostess. She was not very welcoming or pleasant, no hello or you're welcome as we were seated. Once we were leaving she didn't even say thank you for coming. Must have been a bad day. That first impression is very important.."

4. [5.0* on 2017-11-25] "Tammy rocks, I got my girl in last minute and Tammy was able to squeeze her in! Tammy does incredible work, very detailed and caring, really cares about her clients! My girl says her nails came out perfect and is extremely happy! Thanks Tammy!"

5. [5.0* on 2016-10-29] "Street taco has kind of become our downtown tradition, the food here is excellent and prices are reasonable. I recommend the carne asada torta with just the right amount of beans and avocado, very delicious! Chip and salsa bar rock! Great Staff and very clean. See you soon! Support Local! Thanks!"

6. [4.0* on 2015-01-26] "Great internet sales service, bought a good used Pilot from Chapman. Ask for sales manager Ralph Soza, great guy! We are very happy!"

7. [5.0* on 2013-01-30] "Great service and reasonable prices, not shady like other Tow services. I highly recommend!"

8. [4.0* on 2010-07-16] "Me and my lovely other half sometimes need a little getaway, this place is like going back in time..cocktails are great, I prefer the Sierra Nevada pint in a ice cold frosty glass, and she is down for the Appletinis...this is the place, love it!!"

9. [5.0* on 2010-07-16] "I have been going there for about 24 years, Its awesome the hogie lunch special is the bomb! My girl prefers the Suzi sorority, great place definatley a Tucson Landmark!!"

10. [3.0* on 2010-07-13] "This place is great, the food is terrific, love the selection...one problem, today i brought my girlfriend and small child there for lunch and we decided to have salads for lunch, there was older, grouchy lady working on the salad bar, she was not plesant at all, she made us feel like we were getting in her way while she was stocking it with fresh items, ve…"

11. [5.0* on 2010-07-13] "Awesome...what can i say...been going there for years! The hot dogs are the bomb, if youre in the mood for carne asada, have the "burro mediano or grande...damn its loaded with meat, dont forget all the fixins..love it!!"

parameter name="context">USER PROFILE: name=Unknown; review_count=0; yelping_since=2020-01-01 00:00:00; average_stars=3.50; useful=0; funny=0; cool=0; fans=0; elite=none

USER_HISTORICAL_AVERAGE_STARS=3.74
USER_MODAL_STARS: 4.0 (their most frequent rating — strong predictor of next rating)
USER_RATING_VARIANCE: 0.83 (consistent rater — narrow range)

USER_TYPICAL_WORD_COUNT: ~127 words per review (mirror this length in the generated review)
USER_CATEGORY_SPECIFIC_AVERAGE: 3.50 (from 4 past reviews mentioning similar venues — prefer this over overall average when predicting stars)

RECENT REVIEWS:
- [5.0*] So happy with Vicky's work. She stayed late to finish with me and took her time, really paying attention to detail. I never felt rushed!
- [3.0*] Tucked away around the corner from PetSmart, not visible from Hillsborough and definitely not where Google Maps says it is. Smoothie was OK and girl was very kind. I'd return but I don't think their smoothies are anything out of the norm.
- [4.0*] Yummy! I'm a fan. I had a delicious grilled cheese for take out one day- chose Cuban bread with cheddar and gouda. Then returned for brunch on the patio with a friend of mine. Everything was delicious and our server was attentive and very helpful. I will definitely return!
- [4.0*] They made beautiful custom arrangements for my grandmother and mother for Mother's Day. They have gorgeous flowers, many that you can't find elsewhere in Tampa. I love that you can tell them what colors and flowers you like and what your budget is and have them create something new and unique. Can't wait to use them again.
- [5.0*] I followed Luis from their previous location. Who wouldn't follow this man wherever he goes? He puts a smile on my face every time I walk through the door, and it stays on well after I've left. When I arrive the girls at the front set me up with some wine and munchies the Luis comes over with a big smile and a hug.
- [1.0*] The office itself is impressive. I was blown away by the decor and the pre-exam chat with the nurse practitioner in her office. It definitely made it less awkward when we got down to business. And while I had her attention, the experience was great. And I guess a long wait to be brought back is somewhat expected in a doctor's office. Where they fall short...
- [4.0*] So many slices to go! Went one evening on my way home from work. I was feeling "hangry." Picked up some slices for me and the hubby. Everything was delicious but the crust had gotten a little stiffer than I'm used to with a NY slice. It's understandable given the convenience though. I will definitely be back.
- [4.0*] Very nice facility. Much more welcoming in appearance than a hospital waiting room. They were very efficient in taking my information. They called my insurance company and provided me with a paper showing how much my copayment was and that it was verified with my insurance company. It was comforting to know how much I would be liable for that day.
- [2.0*] Went here the other night for dinner. Food was just meh. It seemed like their solution to flavor was just jalapeño. I had the spicy guacamole and Jessica Special with a glass of red wine. My husband said his burger was good but I tried some of his fries and they were overly seasoned with Seasoned Salt. I doubt I would ever return for another meal.
- [4.0*] This review is for the CAFE only. Popped in after mass next door for a glass of wine for me and a fancy coffee and cookie for the husband. Total with tip was about $20. So not terribly pricey but definitely not a bargain night. For that $20 though, he really enjoyed his iced chocolate hazelnut Illy-brand mocha and his chocolate chip cookie. I enjoyed my glass of wine.
- [4.0*] The Fresh Market is on a level all its own. There's nothing quite like it. They're somewhat like Whole Foods but smaller and somehow different. It's produce section is predominantly conventional with a good amount of organic selections. It's prepared foods are delicious. No other "grocery bakery" can really compare to their selection of tarts, pies, cakes.
- [4.0*] Great place to go on a Sunday for a quick farmers market trip. Just make sure to have plenty of cash on hand as most vendors do not accept cards. Go early for the best selection of organic produce from Sweetwater and Little Pond. Try a mushroom "burger"- portobello mushrooms, peppers, and onion sauteed in liquid aminos and served on a sandwich thin with m…</parameter>

OTHER REVIEWERS ABOUT THIS BUSINESS (snippets):
- [4.0*] A very nice little find on davis island. surprisingly good. had lunch there this week and was pleasantly surprised. friend staff. Try the corn fritter as an appatizer as I read in a long forgotten review
- [5.0*] One of the best destinations in Tampa.. Authentic and delicious. Worth checking out if you're nearby Davis Island! Red Curry with chicken was delicious. Mild in spice but very flavorful.
- [5.0*] Best Thai in S Tampa and a cool location. I've tried a number of things on the menu and all of them have been fantastic. You can't go wrong with Thai Island
- [5.0*] This place is amazing. The previous reviews were rather harsh. Good Thai food in this area is scarce. The service is excellent, very quick, even on a busy day. the spicy noodle is yummy and the Tom Yum soup is my absolute favorite!
- [4.0*] I love this place for lunch! Where else can you go for an egg roll, plate of food and a small dessert for under 10$?!?!? Would I say it's the best thai in town... No, but it is way better than the thai place in downtown tampa...</parameter>

output.review: Stopped by The Honey Pot on a recent night out in Ybor. The drag shows are definitely the highlight here and the bartenders keep the drinks coming fast. The club gets packed pretty quickly and can get really loud, which is typical for a dance club but might not be for everyone. If you're looking for a fun night with good entertainment and don't mind the noise, it's worth checking out.

output.review: Thrive Pilates & Yoga is a solid find in Philadelphia. The studio is clean and the instructors are wonderful—encouraging and knowledgeable. My only minor gripe is that classes can get a bit crowded, so booking ahead is a must. Worth a visit!

output.review: I tried a class at Thrive and really enjoyed it. The studio space is clean and inviting, and the instructors are wonderful—knowledgeable and great at offering modifications for different skill levels. My only minor note is that classes can get a bit crowded, which sometimes makes it harder to move freely. Still, I'd definitely come back for another session.

task.description:
This is a simulation task. 
            You are a simulation agent that simulates a user's rating and review with an item. 
            There is a user with id and an item with id.

output.review: Look, I've been to enough Philly dive bars to know what I'm getting into, and McGlinchey's delivers exactly what it promises. Cheap drinks, a bartender who looks like he's been there since Prohibition, and that beautiful haze of smoke that'll make your clothes smell for three days. Their hot dogs are legit solid, the door guy is super chill, and at these prices you can stay until last call without taking out a second mortgage. It's divey in all the right ways, and honestly? I'd rather spend my money here than at some overpriced 'craft' place any day.
